sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge) [OK]

prose: Fukui reactivity indices for pyridine were computed at DFT B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier 'standard'), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1, using Mulliken partial charges in PySCF. The geometry was taken from the user-supplied pyridine.xyz without re-optimization at DFT (single-point Fukui evaluation). All three SCFs (neutral, cation +1/doublet, anion −1/doublet) converged. Integrity: trustworthy=true; charge conservation passed for both f+ and f−. Headline result: most electrophilic atom is N (atom 1), f+ = 0.48422572; most nucleophilic atom is C (atom 4), f− = 0.11826260. The dual descriptor confirms N as the dominant electrophilic centre (Δf = +0.42010). Pyridine ring carbons at positions 3/4 (β-carbons) show the highest nucleophilic indices (f− ≈ 0.118), consistent with known electrophilic substitution preference at the 3-position. All hydrogen atoms carry negative f+ and positive f−, and negative dual descriptor values, reflecting the electron-withdrawing influence of the ring nitrogen.
